rationale: The cited paper appears tightly aligned with the citing paper’s core constructs: both study price-presentation framing effects on choice under economically equivalent prices, and both include buyer-versus-seller role/mind-set reversals as key boundary conditions (citing Study 3; cited Study 3). Given the citing note’s claim that it extends prior price-presentation research and refines mechanism accounts, this citation is plausibly a substantive prior-theory/mechanism source rather than an obvious incidental mention.
